summary: Retested frontend after the tracing-border pointer-events fix. All previously blocked interactions now work with real mouse clicks. Full end-to-end verified: (1) tier-elite-btn clickable (elementFromPoint returns SPAN inside the anchor, not the tracing-border overlay), (2) AI Assessment form — Industry Select opens, sliders adjust, submit fires and Claude Sonnet report renders in [data-testid=assess-result] (2352 chars, score 100/100 for all-5s), (3) Enterprise Intake submit succeeds and record appears in /admin Enterprise Leads (Enterprises count went from 1 -> 2), (4) Provider Application (tier: elite) submit succeeds, record appears in /admin Provider Vetting NEW column, Move dropdown -> approved moves it to APPROVED column, and stat-estimated-arr updates from $5,000 -> $10,000 after refresh. No overlay/interception remaining.
passed: tier-elite-btn is clickable (previously blocked by .tracing-border overlay)
assess-industry Select opens on mouse click; BFSI option selectable
assess-submit click triggers /api/assessment; LLM markdown result renders in [data-testid=assess-result]
ent-size Select opens on mouse click; Enterprise option selectable
ent-submit click triggers /api/enterprise/intake; success toast shown; record appears in /admin Enterprise Leads (TEST_Ent_ visible; Enterprises stat = 2)
prov-tier Select opens on mouse click; Elite · $5,000 option selectable
prov-submit click triggers /api/provider/application; success toast shown; record appears in kanban NEW column
Move dropdown -> approved moves provider to APPROVED column; stat-estimated-arr updates $5,000 -> $10,000 on refresh (elite=$5k per approved)
AI Assessments tab shows the newly created TEST_Acme_ assessment with rendered View report link
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx is 540 lines and mixes 5+ section components (Hero, Tiers, Assessment, Network, Forms, Founder, Footer). Consider splitting into /pages/landing/*.jsx modules for maintainability.

Assessment score is computed on the backend from 4 sliders; frontend shows the LLM-generated markdown verbatim inside a <pre>. Long reports (>2k chars) render without any Markdown formatting (no bold/headings). Consider a react-markdown renderer inside [data-testid=assess-result] for readability.
AdminDashboard uses 4 parallel GETs on every load and again on every Move action; consider optimistic UI updates instead of full reload after status change to avoid the 2s spinner flash.
No confirmation on Move dropdown: an accidental click on 'rejected' or 'approved' is irreversible from the UI. Consider a confirm step for approved/rejected transitions.
Radix Select options rely on visible text (e.g. 'Elite · $5,000') rather than a stable data-testid on SelectItem; adding data-testid to each SelectItem would make selection tests less brittle.
test_credentials: None required — public forms + open /admin (no auth wall).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The tracing-border pointer-events fix in /app/frontend/src/index.css (line 94, .tracing-border::before { pointer-events: none; }) fully resolves the click interception on the Assessment form column and the tier-elite card. All Radix Selects, Sliders, and submit buttons on the landing page are now reachable via real mouse clicks. The stat-estimated-arr aggregation correctly counts $5,000 per approved elite provider.
rca of the issue: Root cause of iter 1 bug: the .tracing-border::before pseudo-element (animated conic-gradient border, absolutely positioned with inset:-1px covering the whole card) was rendered above sibling content in the stacking context. By default, pseudo-elements accept pointer events, so clicks on the underlying Radix SelectTrigger and submit <button> hit the ::before layer instead. Fix: add `pointer-events: none;` to .tracing-border::before so the decorative border no longer captures mouse events while keeping its visual appearance.
